concept.shortdesc: First 50–100 Service Hours or 1 Week: Change engine oil and filter. Check all fluid

conbody.p: First 50–100 Service Hours or 1 Week: Change engine oil and filter. Check all fluid levels. Inspect tyres for damage and inflation pressure. Check battery electrolyte level.
Every 500 Service Hours or 3 Months: Change engine oil and filter. Inspect air cleaner element. Check fuel filter. Inspect cooling system hoses. Lubricate all grease fittings.
Every 1000 Service Hours or 6 Months: Replace fuel filter. Inspect and adjust valve clearances. Check and replace transmission fluid if required. Inspect brakes.
Every 1500 Service Hours or 9 Months: Inspect and service hydraulic system. Check all electrical connections. Inspect mast chains and lubricate. Check carriage rollers.
Every 2000 Service Hours or Yearly: Replace coolant. Inspect and replace hydraulic filter. Complete brake inspection and adjustment. Inspect and replace timing belt.
Every 4500 Service Hours or 36 Months: Replace all rubber hoses. Complete powertrain inspection. Replace hydraulic oil. Inspect and service all safety systems.
Page 29 of 500 | CD100S-7 / CD120S-7 / CD140S-7 / CD160S-7 Operation & Maintenance Manual | DL06P Tier-4
Always follow all safety precautions when operating or maintaining this equipment.
Ensure all operators are properly trained and certified before operating the lift truck.
Regular maintenance is essential to safe and efficient operation of the lift truck.
Contact your local CROWN dealer for parts, service, and technical support.
Keep this manual with the lift truck at all times for reference by operators and technicians.
Record all service and maintenance activities in the maintenance logbook.
Only use genuine CROWN replacement parts to maintain warranty and performance.
